ocr.text: The first table presents data on microsatellite markers, detailing the microsatellite marker name, chromosome, allelic size range
in base pairs, number of alleles per locus (A), PCR positivity, and heterozygosity (HE). For the marker TA1, located on
chromosome 6, the allelic size range is 74–190 bp, with 4 alleles, 60% PCR positivity, and HE of 0.650. Polyα, on chromosome
4, shows an allelic size range of 138–180 bp, 5 alleles, 65% PCR positivity, and HE of 0.687.
Microsatellite marker name  TA1  Polyα  PfPk2  TA109  2490  C2M33  C3M69
Chromosome  6  4  12  6  10  2  3
No. of alleles per locus (A)  4  5  3  2  2  5  5
PCR positivity*  60  65  64  62  60  64  65
Types of neoglottis and phonation closure  Number of cases
One vocal fold and epiglottis  7
Laryngeal surface of epiglottis, 2 arytenoid cartilages and 2 pseudo-folds  7
Laryngeal surface of epiglottis and 2 arytenoid cartilages  1
Scar after epiglittis and 2 aryepiglottic folds  2
Pseudo vocal folds  1
Pseudo- folds without arytenoid cartilages  2
PfPk2, on chromosome 12, has an allelic size range of 160–192 bp, 3 alleles, 64% PCR positivity, and HE of 0.531. TA109, on
chromosome 6, has an allelic size range of 188–200 bp, with 2 alleles, 62% PCR positivity, and HE of 0.550. The marker 2490,
located on chromosome 10, has an allelic size range of 80–84 bp, 2 alleles, 60% PCR positivity, and HE of 0.469. C2M33, on
chromosome 2, has an allelic size range of 140–228 bp, 5 alleles, 64% PCR positivity, and HE of 0.256. Lastly, C3M69, located
on chromosome 3, has an allelic size range of 122–138 bp, 5 alleles, 65% PCR positivity, and HE of 0.636. The second table
outlines types of neoglottis and phonation closure, with the header indicating the number of cases. One vocal fold and epiglottis
accounted for 7 cases, laryngeal surface of epiglottis, 2 arytenoid cartilages and 2 pseudo-folds also accounted for 7 cases,
while laryngeal surface of epiglottis and 2 arytenoid cartilages accounted for 1 case. Scar after epiglottis and 2 aryepiglottic
folds accounted for 2 cases, and pseudo vocal folds represented 1 case, while pseudo-folds without arytenoid cartilages
accounted for 2 cases.
